ddls:
CREATE TABLE "orders" (
  "order_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each order */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who placed the order */,
  "order_status" TEXT /* Status of the order (e.g., confirmed, partially shipped, fulfilled) */,
  "total" REAL /* Total amount of the order */,
  "order_date" TEXT /* Date the order was placed */,
  "view_details" TEXT /* Link to view order details */,
  "shipping_address" TEXT /* Shipping address for the order */,
  "billing_address" TEXT /* Billing address for the order */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used for the order */,
  "shipping_carrier" TEXT /* Shipping carrier for the order */,
  "tracking_number" TEXT /* Tracking number for the order */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("order_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_orders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company or customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City of the customer */,
  "state" TEXT /* State of the customer */,
  "zip" TEXT /* Zip code of the customer */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_status_history" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status update */,
  "order_id" TEXT /* ID of the order being updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* New status of the order */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date the status was updated */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who updated the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_status_history_updated_by FOREIGN KEY ("updated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_status_history_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "order_items" (
  "item_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each order item */,
  "order_id" TEXT /* ID of the order the item belongs to */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product being ordered */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the product being ordered */,
  "unit_price" REAL /* Unit price of the product */,
  "subtotal" REAL /* Subtotal for the order item */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("item_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_order_items_order_id FOREIGN KEY ("order_id") REFERENCES orders ("order_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "stock_level" INTEGER /* Current stock level of the product */,
  "reorder_level" INTEGER /* Reorder level for the product */,
  "reorder_quantity" INTEGER /* Reorder quantity for the product */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., customer service, warehouse staff, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* ID of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)
column_names: ID of the customer who placed the order
Status of the order (e.g., confirmed, partially shipped, fulfilled)
Payment method used for the order
Name of the company or customer
Unique identifier for each status update
ID of the order being updated
ID of the user who updated the status
Unique identifier for each order item
ID of the order the item belongs to
ID of the product being ordered
Quantity of the product being ordered
Current stock level of the product
Role of the user (e.g., customer service, warehouse staff, admin)
